steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank must logically complete the claim that calling Agnes Grey autobiographical 'presents drawbacks,' specifically that readers may believe Hartley 'merely fictionalized true events.' The word 'merely' signals that real events are the whole substance, leaving little room for creative invention. In a field that prizes creativity and inventiveness, this impression would be damaging to Hartley's reputation as an artist.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — *Agnes Grey* is less of an imaginative achievement than it actually is.: directly follows from the logic — if readers think Hartley only transcribed real life, they underestimate her creative achievement. ✓
  • Choice B — the real-world counterparts of other characters in *Agnes Grey* are hard to identify.: identifying source characters is a separate issue; the passage never raises it. ✗
  • Choice C — Hartley should not have claimed that *Agnes Grey* is based on real events.: the passage never says Hartley made such a claim — critics applied the autobiographical label. ✗
  • Choice D — critics disagree about whether *Agnes Grey* shows greater originality than works without autobiographical elements.: no critical disagreement is mentioned anywhere in the passage. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
